heading: Forest Legacy Program

children.heading: Establishment and purpose
children.label: a Establishment and purpose
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program, to be known as the Forest Legacy Program, in cooperation with appropriate State, regional, and other units of government for the purposes of ascertaining and protecting environmentally important forest areas that are threatened by conversion to nonforest uses and, through the use of conservation easements and other mechanisms, for promoting forest land protection and other conservation opportunities. Such purposes shall also include the protection of important scenic, cultural, fish, wildlife, and recreational resources, riparian areas, and other ecological values.

children.heading: State and regional forest legacy programs
children.label: b State and regional forest legacy programs
children.text: The Secretary shall exercise the authority under subsection (a) in conjunction with State or regional programs that the Secretary deems consistent with this section.

children.heading: Interests in land
children.label: c Interests in land
children.text: In addition to the authorities granted under and , the Secretary may acquire from willing landowners lands and interests therein, including conservation easements and rights of public access, for Forest Legacy Program purposes. The Secretary shall not acquire conservation easements with title held in common ownership with any other entity.section 515 of this titlesection 2268a(a) of title 7

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Lands and interests therein acquired under subsection (c) may be held in perpetuity for program and easement administration purposes as the Secretary may provide. In administering lands and interests therein under the program, the Secretary shall identify the environmental values to be protected by entry of the lands into the program, management activities which are planned and the manner in which they may affect the values identified, and obtain from the landowner other information determined appropriate for administration and management purposes.

children.heading: Initial programs
children.label: 2 Initial programs
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish a regional program in furtherance of the Northern Forest Lands Study in the States of New York, New Hampshire, Vermont, and Maine under . The Secretary shall establish additional programs in each of the Northeast, Midwest, South, and Western regions of the United States, and the Pacific Northwest (including the State of Washington), on the preparation of an assessment of the need for such programs.November 28, 1991Public Law 100–446

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: e Eligibility
children.text: Not later than , and in consultation with State Forest Stewardship Coordinating Committees established under and similar regional organizations, the Secretary shall establish eligibility criteria for the designation of forest areas from which lands may be entered into the Forest Legacy Program and subsequently select such appropriate areas. To be eligible, such areas shall have significant environmental values or shall be threatened by present or future conversion to nonforest uses. Of land proposed to be included in the Forest Legacy Program, the Secretary shall give priority to lands which can be effectively protected and managed, and which have important scenic or recreational values; riparian areas; fish and wildlife values, including threatened and endangered species; or other ecological values.November 28, 1991section 2113(b) of this title

children.heading: Application
children.label: f Application
children.text: For areas included in the Forest Legacy Program, an owner of lands or interests in lands who wishes to participate may prepare and submit an application at such time in such form and containing such information as the Secretary may prescribe. The Secretary shall give reasonable advance notice for the submission of all applications to the State forester, equivalent State official, or other appropriate State or regional natural resource management agency. If applications exceed the ability of the Secretary to fund them, priority shall be given to those forest areas having the greatest need for protection pursuant to the criteria described in subsection (e).

children.heading: State consent
children.label: g State consent
children.text: Where a State has not approved the acquisition of land under , the Secretary shall not acquire lands or interests therein under authority granted by this section outside an area of that State designated as a part of a program established under subsection (b).section 515 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Conservation easements or deed reservations acquired or reserved pursuant to this section may allow forest management activities, including timber management, on areas entered in the Forest Legacy Program insofar as the Secretary deems such activities consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Assignment of responsibilities
children.label: 2 Assignment of responsibilities
children.text: For Forest Legacy Program areas, the Secretary may delegate or assign management and enforcement responsibilities over federally owned lands and interests in lands only to another governmental entity.

children.heading: Duties of owners
children.label: i Duties of owners
children.text: Under the terms of a conservation easement or other property interest acquired under subsection (b), the landowner shall be required to manage property in a manner that is consistent with the purposes for which the land was entered in the Forest Legacy Program and shall not convert such property to other uses. Hunting, fishing, hiking, and similar recreational uses shall not be considered inconsistent with the purposes of this program.1So in original. Probably should be “subsection (c),”.1

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 1 Compensation
children.text: The Secretary shall pay the fair market value of any property interest acquired under this section. Payments under this section shall be in accordance with Federal appraisal and acquisition standards and procedures.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: 2 Cost sharing
children.text: In accordance with terms and conditions that the Secretary shall prescribe, costs for the acquisition of lands or interests therein or project costs shall be shared among participating entities including regional organizations, State and other governmental units, landowners, corporations, or private organizations. Such costs may include, but are not limited to, those associated with planning, administration, property acquisition, and property management. To the extent practicable, the Federal share of total program costs shall not exceed 75 percent, including any in-kind contribution.

children.heading: Reserved interest deeds
children.label: 1 Reserved interest deeds
children.text: As used in this section, the term “conservation easement” includes an easement utilizing a reserved interest deed where the grantee acquires all rights, title, and interests in a property, except those rights, title, and interests that may run with the land that are expressly reserved by a grantor.

children.label: A
children.text: the conservation easement being in gross or appurtenant;

children.label: B
children.text: the management of the conservation easement having been delegated or assigned to a non-Federal entity;

children.label: C
children.text: any requirement under State law for re-recordation or renewal of the easement; or

children.label: D
children.text: any future disestablishment of a Forest Legacy Program area or other Federal project for which the conservation easement was originally acquired.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 3 Construction
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of State law, conservation easements shall be construed to effect the Federal purposes for which they were acquired and, in interpreting their terms, there shall be no presumption favoring the conservation easement holder or fee owner.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, at the request of a participating State, provide a grant to the State to carry out the Forest Legacy Program in the State.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary shall use a portion of the funds made available under subsection (m), as determined by the Secretary, to provide a grant to the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: the State shall use the grant to carry out the Forest Legacy Program in the State, including the acquisition by the State of lands and interests in lands.

children.heading: Transfer of Forest Legacy Program land—
children.label: 3 Transfer of Forest Legacy Program land—

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to any terms and conditions that the Secretary may require (including the requirements described in subparagraph (B)), the Secretary may, at the request of the State of Vermont, convey to the State, by quitclaim deed, without consideration, any land or interest in land acquired in the State under the Forest Legacy Program.

children.label: I
children.text: conserves the land or interest in land; and

children.label: II
children.text: is consistent with any other Forest Legacy Program purposes for which the land or interest in land was acquired;

children.label: I
children.text: reimburse the Secretary in an amount that is based on the current market value of the land or interest in land in proportion to the amount of consideration paid by the United States for the land or interest in land; or

children.label: II
children.text: convey to the Secretary land or an interest in land that is equal in value to the land or interest in land conveyed.

children.heading: Disposition of funds
children.label: C Disposition of funds
children.text: Amounts received by the Secretary under subparagraph (B)(ii) shall be credited to the Wildland Fire Management account, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: m Appropriation
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.
